evidence.scientific_clarity: Violin+box+jitter overlay clearly shows distributions for all 4 conditions in the correct Control→Drug-A 10µM→Drug-A 50µM→Combo order; significance brackets with Bonferroni correction communicate the key comparisons.
evidence.typography: PDF uses Type 3 (bitmap) DejaVuSans fonts (no fonttype=42 in script); font is the Matplotlib default DejaVuSans, not a publication-quality typeface; text sizes (9–11 pt) are reasonable but the missing fonttype=42 is a hard failure.
evidence.palette: Palette (#4C72B0, #55A868, #C44E52, #8172B2) is the Seaborn deep palette—reasonably CB-safe for deuteranopia but not fully tested; red (#C44E52) is used for Drug-A 50µM (a dose, not a direction), which is borderline for green/red directional discipline.
evidence.layout_density: 7×5 inch figure uses space well; tight_layout applied; no overlapping labels; y-axis range is data-driven; significance brackets stack cleanly without crowding.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure contents exactly including Bonferroni correction and significance thresholds; no legend needed (x-tick labels suffice); overall presentation is ready for submission.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF fonts are Type 3 (rendered as bitmaps), not Type 1/TrueType (fonttype=42); text is NOT selectable in the PDF; script never sets matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42.
evidence.file_format: All four required files exist and are non-trivial: figure.pdf (28 KB), figure.png (85 KB), caption.tex (461 B), gen_figure.py (3.2 KB); bonus figure.svg also present.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption identifies plot type, n=100 per group, explains violin/box/jitter layers, states alpha=0.35, names the statistical test (Mann-Whitney U with Bonferroni for 5 comparisons), and gives significance thresholds—a complete, informative caption.

overall_strengths: Complete, scientifically clear figure with violin+box+jitter overlay, correct condition ordering, and significance brackets computed with appropriate multiple-testing correction.
Caption is thorough and self-contained, matching every visual element in the figure and providing numerical/statistical specifics reviewers expect.
overall_weaknesses: PDF font type is Type 3 (bitmap) because fonttype=42 was never set; text is not selectable/searchable in the PDF, a common journal rejection criterion.
Default DejaVuSans font and no explicit rcParams font configuration—publication-quality figures typically use Helvetica, Arial, or a similar sans-serif via fonttype=42.
